stub: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white middle class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She frequently speaks out and votes against her party leadership.
substantive: She supports the construction of thousands of new onshore wind turbines.
justification: In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I always listen to voters’ views, because at the end of the day they get to choose whether to reelect me or not.”
text: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white middle class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She frequently speaks out and votes against her party leadership. She supports the construction of thousands of new onshore wind turbines. In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I always listen to voters’ views, because at the end of the day they get to choose whether to reelect me or not.”
